exact_route: ByteTagSparse.lookup performs group match, validates FULL at the selected slot, invokes equality on that payload only, and terminates on EMPTY.
required_state: Stable root, version, control region, payload region, capacity, and closed ByteTagSparse profile.
provenance: A profile-specific Hit token binds root, version, slot, and payload place; hash and integer index grant nothing.
failure_cleanup: Hash or equality exceptional exit leaves the table unchanged.
fact_behavior: Profile-specific GroupCandidates, Occupied, KeyHit, and ProbeStop facts have mutation and root invalidators.
reference_events: Reference group loads, tag tests, equality calls, and probe advance only.
safety_status: All paper obligations enumerated; no formal proof.
growth_convergence_risk: Every new control encoding duplicates lookup and fact rules; remains C but has high catalog-growth risk.
unresolved: Generated code shape and formal fact soundness.
falsifier: A second sparse representation requires an unbounded or project-named lookup variant.

exact_route: ByteTagSparse.find_vacant issues a profile-specific VacantEntry token; insert_no_grow and insert_grow perform fixed commits.
required_state: Stable or Grow phase plus selected EMPTY or DELETED slot and exact offered owners.
owner_traffic: No-grow moves key and value once; grow stages each old payload once and moves the offered pair once.
provenance: VacantEntry binds root, version, slot, old control class, and offered owners; result borrow re-roots to the committed value place.
failure_cleanup: Precommit call or allocation failure preserves old table and offered owners; postcommit has fixed completion and no escaped token.
fact_behavior: Vacancy facts invalidate on commit; new Occupied and result facts publish at the new version or root.
c0_dependency: ROOT-ALLOC-1 for growth; GROUP-MATCH-1 for probe.
reference_events: Reference probe, one payload write, control/counter updates, and only the reference growth allocation and relocations.
safety_status: All paper owner outcomes enumerated; no formal proof.
boundedness: Finite ByteTagSparse entry and growth rules.
growth_convergence_risk: Insertion sequencing is repeated for every profile and entry form; high catalog-growth risk.
unresolved: Formal logical-commit refinement over physical write order.
falsifier: A required no-grow insertion needs an extra persistent tag, rollback scan, or payload copy.

exact_route: ByteTagSparse.replace and replace_with define duplicate-key retention, displaced-value return, and the callback Some or None branches.
required_state: Stable occupied slot or profile-specific Replace phase.
owner_traffic: Ordinary replacement consumes offered key, installs new value once, and returns old value; callback branch takes one owner and restores or removes it.
provenance: OccupiedEntry and returned borrow rules are profile-specific and version-bounded; owned results carry no table borrow.
failure_cleanup: Pre-match failure changes nothing; profile rule fixes callback exceptional cleanup and both branch outcomes.
fact_behavior: Occupancy may transfer only in the restore branch; content, borrow, and key-hit facts invalidate at transition entry.
c0_dependency: None beyond GROUP-MATCH-1 for the preceding lookup.
reference_events: Reference equality, one owner exchange or take, optional restore, and no allocation.
safety_status: All paper dispositions enumerated; no formal proof.
growth_convergence_risk: Callback and result variants multiply the profile operation catalog; high catalog-growth risk.
falsifier: One owner can be observed live both in the slot and callback or lacks a unique disposition.

exact_route: ByteTagSparse.remove moves the owner out, runs the profile's EMPTY-versus-DELETED rule, and consumes the occupied token.
required_state: Stable occupied slot and neighboring group summaries under one version.
owner_traffic: Stored key and value move once to the result; no payload drop in place.
provenance: OccupiedEntry binds exact slot; consumption invalidates all derived references; result is owned.
failure_cleanup: Miss changes nothing; found removal completes without recoverable post-take failure.
fact_behavior: Old Occupied and probe facts invalidate; profile-specific tombstone and growth facts publish together.
reference_events: Reference owner read, two group observations, control update, and counter updates only.
safety_status: All paper transition obligations enumerated; no formal proof.
growth_convergence_risk: Deletion reachability rules repeat for each probing profile; high catalog-growth risk.
unresolved: Formal proof that the profile tombstone rule preserves probe reachability.
falsifier: Preserving lookup requires an extra scan, metadata bit, or profile-name exception.

exact_route: ByteTagSparse.resize and rehash_in_place explicitly enumerate staged-byte relocation, transition-DELETED live interpretation, restore, move, swap, commit, and partial cleanup.
required_state: Resize old/new roots and progress, or an exclusive profile-specific Rehash phase with pending-live slots.
owner_traffic: Resize stages each live payload once and transfers liveness at commit; in-place branch restores, moves, or swaps one accounted owner at a time.
provenance: All old tokens invalidate at phase entry; resize survivors re-root at commit; in-place survivors receive the new same-root version.
failure_cleanup: Allocation failure preserves old owners; resize abort discards non-owner staged bytes; in-place exceptional exit destroys each pending-live owner once and preserves processed FULL owners.
fact_behavior: Pre-rehash facts invalidate; phase facts do not escape; final occupancy, probe, growth, and root facts publish only at commit.
reference_events: Reference one growth allocation for resize, one staged relocation per live payload, old-root release, or reference in-place scan/move/swap/cleanup.
safety_status: All paper phases and cleanup outcomes enumerated; no formal proof.
boundedness: Finite but large profile-specific rehash automaton.
growth_convergence_risk: Every representation duplicates the largest state machine; highest catalog and review-growth risk.
unresolved: Formal validation of staged-byte non-ownership and partial-progress cleanup.
falsifier: A new sparse profile cannot reuse rehash without project recognition or another full operation catalog.

exact_route: SPARSE-AUTOMATON-1 uses the selected profile's closed control classifier, slot map, group summary, and probe transition to issue candidates, Occupied tokens, and ProbeStop.
required_state: Stable(profile, root, version) with compiler-known control classes and checked regions.
provenance: Occupied(root, version, slot, place) is produced only by the automaton's live-class rule; KeyHit adds equality evidence but no new place authority.
failure_cleanup: Behavior-call exceptional exit preserves Stable unchanged.
fact_behavior: Generic fixed fact kinds are parameterized only by compiler-known profile ID, root, version, and slot; listed mutations invalidate them.
reference_events: Reference group loads, masks, equality calls, and probe transition only.
safety_status: All paper rules enumerated; no formal proof.
boundedness: One closed automaton schema and finite compiler-owned profile table.
growth_convergence_risk: New profiles add data rows only if they fit the schema; schema extension remains a language change, so it stays distinct from B and A.
unresolved: Formal automaton and fact-schema proof; evidence from a second independent sparse demand.
falsifier: A required control relation cannot be expressed without a writer predicate or a new transition kind.

exact_route: SPARSE-AUTOMATON-1 issues Vacant(root, version, slot, old_class); INSERT-1 consumes it through prepare, logical commit, counter transfer, and result publication.
required_state: Stable plus non-escapable Insert phase; optional Resize phase for growth.
owner_traffic: No-grow moves offered owners once; resize stages each old payload once and commits one offered pair.
provenance: Vacant and result tokens carry exact root/version/slot/place; growth re-roots only at successful commit.
failure_cleanup: Precommit call or ROOT-ALLOC-1 failure returns unchanged table and offered owners; abandonment before commit has fixed owner return; commit is exact-use.
fact_behavior: Vacancy and probe facts invalidate at phase entry; Occupied and result facts publish only at successful commit.
c0_dependency: ROOT-ALLOC-1 for growth and GROUP-MATCH-1 for probing.
reference_events: Exactly the reference probe, payload write, control/counter changes, and conditional growth events.
safety_status: All paper owner, commit, and abandonment rules enumerated; no formal proof.
boundedness: INSERT-1 is one finite template instantiated only by admitted profiles.
growth_convergence_risk: Bounded template/profile cross-product; no ordinary open composition.
unresolved: Formal refinement of hidden intermediate writes and exact counter equations.
falsifier: Supporting EMPTY and DELETED insertion requires a persistent phase tag or an unlisted cleanup program.

exact_route: REPLACE-1 consumes Occupied and offered owners, selecting fixed keep-key value exchange, callback restore, or callback remove outcomes.
required_state: Stable plus non-escapable Replace phase with one taken owner and a closed outcome enum.
owner_traffic: Each offered, retained, displaced, returned, restored, or removed owner has one table-driven disposition.
provenance: Owned result detaches; restored entry and borrow tokens are reissued at the new version from the exact slot place.
failure_cleanup: Pre-match failure is unchanged; declared behavior outcome selects one fixed cleanup/commit row; abort produces no subsequent access authority.
fact_behavior: Occupancy survives only rows that restore FULL; content and borrow facts always invalidate; removal rows transfer to vacancy/probe facts.
reference_events: Reference equality and one owner exchange, take, restore, or removal; no allocation.
safety_status: All paper outcome rows enumerated; no formal proof.
boundedness: One closed REPLACE-1 outcome table shared by admitted sparse profiles.
growth_convergence_risk: Adds a bounded template rather than an operation copy per profile; remains C because libraries cannot add outcomes.
unresolved: Formal callable-effect validation and whether recoverable callback failure is needed beyond the frozen slice.
falsifier: An admitted profile needs a replacement outcome outside the closed owner-disposition table.

exact_route: REMOVE-1 consumes Occupied, moves the owner out, asks the profile's finite erase classifier for EMPTY or DELETED, and commits counters and facts.
required_state: Stable occupied slot plus checked group summaries and one Remove phase.
owner_traffic: Stored owner moves once to the result.
provenance: Removal consumes all slot tokens; result is owned; no same-address fact survives the version change.
failure_cleanup: Miss preserves Stable; after owner take, the fixed transition has no recoverable branch and must complete.
fact_behavior: Occupied and reachability facts invalidate; selected vacant class, growth, and probe facts publish at commit.
reference_events: Reference owner read, group masks, control update, and counter changes only.
safety_status: All paper transition rules enumerated; no formal proof.
boundedness: REMOVE-1 plus a finite per-profile erase-classifier row.
growth_convergence_risk: Profile row growth is bounded by admitted control/probe forms; no user-supplied reachability predicate.
unresolved: Formal proof of each admitted erase classifier.
falsifier: A profile's correct erase choice needs arbitrary program logic, a whole-table scan, or an extra metadata bit.

exact_route: RELOCATE-1 and REHASH-1 define resize staging and liveness transfer plus phase-local pending-live interpretation, restore, move, swap, commit, and partial cleanup.
required_state: Exclusive Resize(old,new,progress) or Rehash(root,version,phase,progress,displaced) state; transition classes are compiler-known.
owner_traffic: Resize stages each live payload once then transfers liveness atomically; in-place transitions keep exactly one owner per slot or displaced token.
provenance: All old access invalidates at entry; successful resize re-roots, in-place reissues same-root new-version tokens, and phase tokens never escape.
failure_cleanup: ROOT-ALLOC-1 failure is precommit; staged bytes are discarded without drop; REHASH-1 exceptional cleanup destroys pending-live owners once and preserves processed FULL owners.
fact_behavior: Only phase facts authorize pending-live payloads; final facts publish after exact completion and all pre-phase facts remain invalid.
reference_events: Exactly the reference allocation/relocation/release or in-place scan/restore/move/swap/cleanup events.
safety_status: All paper phases, invariants, and cleanup rows enumerated; no formal proof.
boundedness: Two fixed templates over finite profile transitions.
growth_convergence_risk: Largest checker table but bounded; adding arbitrary transition programs would converge to B/A and is forbidden.
unresolved: Formal proof of loop invariants, staged-byte validity, and partial-progress cleanup; later independent profile evidence.
falsifier: A required sparse rehash cannot fit the finite phase and transition enum without a structural tax.

exact_route: A library composes generic ControlRelation, GroupView, Probe, PlaceAccess, and FactProducer components to describe lookup.
required_state: Writer-selected relation and component composition over one root.
owner_traffic: No owner traffic if the composition is valid.
provenance: Generic PlaceAccess derives a payload place from the supplied relation.
failure_cleanup: Generic behavior-effect closure preserves the relation on failure.
c0_dependency: A generic group leaf comparable to GROUP-MATCH-1.
reference_events: Can match reference events on paper.
forced_delta: No runtime delta identified, but authority comes from open composition.
safety_status: Potentially safe only after validating an open composition calculus.
boundedness: Finite components but an open cross-product selected by ordinary libraries.
growth_convergence_risk: This is Candidate B's orthogonal algebra; allowing arbitrary predicates further converges to A.
unresolved: A bounded public admission rule for arbitrary sparse relations.
falsifier: Closing composition without B/A authority requires adding profile-specific compiler recognition.

exact_route: A library composes generic Vacancy, ExactUse, Initialize, Commit, ReRoot, and ResultBorrow components.
required_state: Writer-composed transition graph and cleanup relation.
owner_traffic: Can describe one offered-owner move and staged growth relocation.
provenance: Generic tokens derive from the supplied root/place relation.
failure_cleanup: Library-selected commit and cleanup graph accounts for failure and abandonment.
fact_behavior: Generic facts transfer through the composed graph.
reference_events: Can match reference events on paper.
forced_delta: No runtime delta identified, but semantic authority depends on open composition.
safety_status: Potentially safe only after proving the general component calculus.
boundedness: Open composition admits unbounded algorithms despite a finite primitive vocabulary.
growth_convergence_risk: Converges to B; arbitrary cleanup or relation proof converges to A.
unresolved: Exact rejection boundary for malformed transition graphs.
falsifier: Restricting graphs enough for local checking excludes the Hashbrown growth path or recreates SR-PROFILE.

exact_route: A library composes Take, Replace, Return, Restore, Remove, CallbackEffect, and ExactCleanup components.
owner_traffic: Can express all observed owner dispositions.
provenance: Generic result maps refer to composed place identities.
failure_cleanup: Library graph selects exceptional and abandonment cleanup.
fact_behavior: Generic invalidators attach to graph edges.
c0_dependency: Generic group leaf for lookup only.
forced_delta: No runtime delta identified, but each new outcome graph extends semantic composition.
safety_status: Potentially safe only under a general graph verifier.
boundedness: Finite nodes with open graph topology and outcome set.
growth_convergence_risk: Converges to B; proof-carrying arbitrary callbacks push toward A.
unresolved: Termination, exact-use, diagnostic, and graph-equivalence rules.
falsifier: A finite fixed graph catalog suffices without turning back into SR-PROFILE.

exact_route: A library composes Take, ControlWrite, ReachabilityPreserve, CounterTransfer, and Return components.
required_state: Writer-selected erase relation over group summaries.
provenance: Generic occupied token is consumed through the relation.
failure_cleanup: Composed graph has no post-take failure edge.
fact_behavior: Generic reachability facts follow writer-supplied relation.
reference_events: Can match reference group tests and control update.
forced_delta: No runtime delta identified, but reachability authority is open.
safety_status: Potentially safe only after validating arbitrary erase relations.
boundedness: Open relation space even with finite transition primitives.
growth_convergence_risk: Converges to B; a general proof of reachability is A-like.
unresolved: Public admission without project recognition, scans, or general theorem proving.
falsifier: A fixed local relation schema covers all needed erase policies without becoming SR-PROFILE.

exact_route: A library composes Partition, StageBytes, Relocate, Swap, PhaseClass, LoopInvariant, PartialCleanup, ReRoot, and Commit components.
required_state: Writer-defined phase graph, loop invariant, and cleanup traversal.
owner_traffic: Can describe the reference traffic if the invariant is accepted.
provenance: Generic phase and root tokens derive from composed relations.
failure_cleanup: Library-supplied partial-progress cleanup destroys the set named by its invariant.
fact_behavior: Generic fact transfer consumes writer-defined phase proofs.
reference_events: Can match reference allocation, move, swap, and cleanup events.
forced_delta: No runtime delta identified, but the required semantic proof is open-ended.
safety_status: Requires a general transition/invariant verifier rather than a closed family table.
boundedness: Unbounded graph, loop, and invariant space.
growth_convergence_risk: Converges to B immediately and to A if arbitrary invariants are proof-indexed.
unresolved: Checker decidability, proof size, diagnostics, termination, and AI reliability.
falsifier: A finite component grammar expresses rehash without either excluding a branch or recreating REHASH-1.
